extra.message:
Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) via Direct String Concatenation

The application directly concatenates user input (username parameter) into a Jinja2 template string without proper sanitization or parameterization. This allows attackers to inject arbitrary Jinja2 template expressions that will be executed server-side, leading to remote code execution.

Impact: An attacker can achieve remote code execution on the server by injecting malicious Jinja2 template syntax. For example, using payloads like '{{config}}' to access application configuration, '{{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}}' to enumerate Python classes, or more complex payloads to execute arbitrary system commands and read sensitive files.

Fix: Use proper Jinja2 template parameterization as shown in the commented safe implementation. Replace the vulnerable line with: output = Jinja2.from_string('Welcome {{ user_name }}!').render(user_name=username). This ensures user input is treated as data rather than executable template code.
